ocr.text: The analysis of pedigree inbreeding within the context of livestock breeding has become increasingly significant, particularly as
it relates to the genetic diversity and overall health of animal populations. The accompanying table presents a detailed
examination of inbreeding metrics, focusing on key indicators such as SNPRM and ROHRM.
Breed  Diversity parametera  Relationship used to constrain parental relationshipsb
Random  A  SNPRM  ROHRM
These metrics provide insights into the degree of inbreeding present in the studied livestock, with the values associated with
pedigree inbreeding reflecting an average of 0.011 (±0.001). This figure suggests a moderate level of inbreeding within the
population, which can have implications for genetic diversity and the potential for inbreeding depression. Additionally, the data
indicates fluctuations in inbreeding values, evident from the negative and positive deviations recorded under ROHRM, with
values of −0.001 (±0.001) and 0.004 (±0.001), respectively. Such variations underscore the importance of continuous
monitoring and assessment of inbreeding levels to ensure effective breeding strategies that prioritize genetic health and
sustainability. The findings presented in the table serve as a foundational reference for further research and discussion on the
implications of inbreeding within livestock populations.
